title: Sweet Savory Chinese Gruyere Sweet And Sour Skillet
description: A lacto-vegetarian-friendly dish featuring Gruyere, yuzu-cure, and a sweet and sour flavor profile, inspired by Chinese Xinjiang cuisine, cooked to a jammy and aerated perfection in a cast-iron skillet.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the cubed tofu and cook until golden brown on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the tofu from the skillet and set aside on a plate.
In the same skillet, add the chopped scallions and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
In a small bowl, whisk together the yuzu juice, rice vinegar, sugar, ketchup, and cornstarch.
Pour the yuzu mixture into the skillet and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the sauce for about 5 minutes, or until it thickens to a jammy consistency.
Stir in the shredded Gruyere cheese until melted and smooth.
Add the cooked tofu back into the skillet and toss to coat with the sweet and sour sauce.
Serve hot, garnished with additional chopped scallions if desired.
